message:
feat(prune): add interactive review, apply, and split proposal support

Major enhancement to the prune command adding interactive workflow
support and skill splitting capabilities.

## New Subcommands

### ms prune review [--no-prompt]

Interactive review of generated proposals (merge/deprecate/split):
- Displays proposals one at a time with full context
- Prompts for accept/skip/edit decision
- Supports --no-prompt for CI/automation (print-only mode)
- Generates draft skill files for accepted proposals

### ms prune apply <proposal> [--approve] [--dry-run]

Apply a specific proposal by spec:
- merge:skill_a,skill_b - Merge two skills into target
- deprecate:skill [--replacement <id>] - Mark skill deprecated
- split:skill - Execute a split proposal

Requires --approve flag for safety (or --dry-run to preview).

## Split Proposals

New proposal type for skills that have grown too large:

- Triggered when a skill exceeds --split-min-sections (default: 6)
- Generates child skill drafts with logical section groupings
- Limits children via --split-max-children (default: 3)
- Produces draft markdown files for review before execution

## Internal Refactoring

- Extract ProposalSet struct to centralize proposal management
- Add SplitProposal and SplitChildDraft types
- Refactor build_proposals() to return structured ProposalSet
- Add draft_path tracking for merge/split proposals
- Support --dry-run at global level for all subcommands

## Dependencies

Now imports from crate::core::spec_lens for markdown parsing when
generating split drafts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
